file_analyses.file_purpose: YAML test case definition for validating the Instant Services Printer Details modal functionality. This test case covers multiple test IDs (C31165177, C31266422, C30351306, C30351309, C30351310) and validates the printer status pane, printer details modal structure including left and right sections, HP Connected Account hyperlink functionality, and navigation back to overview page. The test uses AI-powered visual verification with strict validation prompts to ensure UI elements are correctly positioned and displayed.

variables_and_constants.constant_value: Test Instant Services Printer details modal

test_case_metadata.description: Test Instant Services Printer details modal

setup_phase.purpose: Performs login to Instant Services application as test precondition

verification_criteria: Word 'Status' positioned at top left after navigation pane
HP printer model name below 'Status'
Printer readiness information and 'Printer Details' present
steps_phase.what_it_does: Performs AI-powered visual verification of the Status pane on the Dashboard. Takes a screenshot and validates that the Status section is correctly displayed with printer model name and Printer Details link. Uses strict binary validation mode where any missing element causes immediate failure.
Clicks on the 'Printer Details' link to open the printer details modal dialog.

verification_criteria: Modal title 'My Printer' at top left
HP printer icon below 'My Printer'
HP Connected Account hyperlink below printer icon
Printer enrolled status below HP connected account hyperlink
steps_phase.what_it_does: Performs AI-powered visual verification of the left section of the Printer Details modal. Validates the presence and positioning of 'My Printer' title, printer icon, HP Connected Account hyperlink, and enrollment status. Uses strict validation where all elements must be present.

verification_criteria: Optional Printer Email with email id
Printer Serial Number with serial number
Service Enrolled Date in month, date, year format
HP Connected Account hyperlink
Printer enrolled status
steps_phase.what_it_does: Performs AI-powered visual verification of the right section of the Printer Details modal. Validates printer email (optional), serial number, service enrolled date format, HP Connected Account hyperlink, and enrollment status. All required elements must be present for validation to pass.

steps_phase.label: HP Connected Account
steps_phase.what_it_does: Clicks on the 'HP Connected Account' hyperlink within the printer details modal to navigate to the HP Connected Account page or section.

steps_phase.label: Overview
steps_phase.what_it_does: Clicks on the 'Overview' menu item in the navigation to return to the Overview page. Waits 30 seconds for page load and any promotional content to appear.

Clicks on the 'HP Instant Ink' navigation item with expand flag set to True, which expands the HP Instant Ink submenu to show Overview, Update Plan, Print and Payment History, and Shipment Tracking options.
Presses the Escape key to close any promotional modal or popup that may have appeared after navigation. Waits 3 seconds for the modal to close.
Clicks the 'Dismiss' button to close any emergency banner notification that may be displayed on the page. Waits 3 seconds after dismissal.

verification_criteria: Word 'Overview' at top left after navigation pane
'instant ink' appears to the right of 'Overview'
'Select printer' dropdown selector next to 'instant ink'
Dropdown contains printer icon and printer model
'Enroll or replace a printer' hyperlink below dropdown
steps_phase.what_it_does: Performs comprehensive AI-powered visual verification of the Overview page layout after returning from printer details. Validates the page title, instant ink logo, printer selector dropdown with icon and model, and the enroll/replace printer hyperlink. All elements must be correctly positioned for validation to pass.
Presses the Escape key to close the printer details modal if it is still open. Waits 5 seconds to ensure the modal is fully closed before proceeding to cleanup.

cleanup_phase.purpose: Performs logout from Instant Services application as test cleanup

key_attributes: Printer icon and model in dropdown
Enroll or replace a printer hyperlink

reusable_test_patterns.pattern_name: AI Visual Verification with Strict Validation
reusable_test_patterns.description: Uses AI-powered screenshot analysis with strict binary validation prompts. Returns {status: false} immediately if any condition fails. Used for validating complex UI layouts and element positioning.
Opens modal by clicking link, validates modal content sections (left and right), interacts with modal elements, then closes modal with Escape key.
Navigates to page, handles promotional popups with Escape key, dismisses emergency banners, then validates page content.
Uses explicit sleep delays after actions ranging from 1 to 30 seconds depending on expected page load time and content rendering.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a comprehensive test case for the Printer Details modal functionality in the HP Instant Ink service. The test demonstrates advanced AI-powered visual verification using strict validation prompts that enforce binary pass/fail outcomes. The test flow includes modal interaction patterns, navigation handling, and cleanup of promotional content. Notable features include: (1) Multiple test case IDs covered in a single YAML file (C31165177, C31266422, C30351306, C30351309, C30351310), (2) Extensive use of AI visual verification with detailed validation criteria embedded in prompts, (3) Handling of dynamic content like promotional modals and emergency banners, (4) Validation of both left and right sections of the modal separately for granular failure detection, (5) Long sleep times (30 seconds) after navigation suggesting potential performance issues or heavy page content. The test uses file references for setup (instantservices_login.yaml) and cleanup (instantservices_logout.yaml) indicating a modular test design pattern. The verification prompts use f-string formatting suggesting they may be templated with dynamic values at runtime.

file_analyses.file_purpose: YAML test case definition for validating the HP Instant Ink navigation menu structure and content layout across all major sections. This test case (C31165181) systematically navigates through each HP Instant Ink submenu item (Overview, Update Plan, Print and Payment History, Shipment Tracking) and validates the page layout, header structure, and key content elements using AI-powered visual verification. Ensures consistent layout patterns across all HP Instant Ink pages.

reusable_test_patterns.pattern_name: Systematic Page Navigation and Layout Validation
reusable_test_patterns.description: Navigates through each page in a section systematically, validating consistent header layout patterns (title, logo, dropdown selector) across all pages.
All HP Instant Ink pages follow the same header layout: page title on left, instant ink logo to the right, Select printer dropdown, and page-specific content below.

reusable_test_patterns.pattern_name: Long Wait Times for Page Loads
reusable_test_patterns.description: Uses 20-30 second wait times after navigation clicks, suggesting pages have significant content loading or performance considerations.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test case provides comprehensive validation of the HP Instant Ink section navigation structure and layout consistency. It systematically visits each of the four main HP Instant Ink pages (Overview, Update Plan, Print and Payment History, Shipment Tracking) and validates that they all follow a consistent header layout pattern. The test demonstrates: (1) Navigation menu expansion and submenu validation, (2) Consistent use of AI visual verification for layout validation across multiple pages, (3) Long wait times (20-30 seconds) after navigation suggesting potential performance issues or data-heavy pages, (4) Validation of consistent UI patterns across different pages (title positioning, logo placement, dropdown presence), (5) Focus on structural layout validation rather than detailed content validation. The test uses modular design with file references for login and logout. The verification prompts are structured to validate relative positioning and presence of elements rather than pixel-perfect matching. This test is valuable for regression testing to ensure UI consistency is maintained across the HP Instant Ink section after updates or changes.

file_analyses.file_purpose: YAML test case definition for validating the Plan Details card content and layout on the HP Instant Ink Overview page. This test case covers test IDs C31165183 and C31266443, validating the structure and content of the Plan Details section including subscription ID, plan information (price, page count), billing and shipping details, and action hyperlinks (Redeem Promo, Transfer Subscription, Cancel Instant Ink). Uses AI-powered visual verification with vertical scrolling to validate content across the entire card.

reusable_test_patterns.description: Validates content in multiple stages by taking screenshots at different scroll positions. First validates upper content, then scrolls, then validates lower content. Useful for validating cards or sections that extend beyond initial viewport.
Validates a card component by checking header, main content sections, and action links in a systematic top-to-bottom approach.
Validation criteria includes optional elements (like 'remaining free months may be present') that don't cause failure if absent, allowing for different user states.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test case focuses specifically on the Plan Details card component on the Overview page. It demonstrates a sophisticated validation approach that handles content extending beyond the viewport by using vertical scrolling between verification steps. The test validates: (1) Card header with Plan Details title and Subscription ID, (2) Plan information including price and page count with optional free months, (3) Billing and Shipping details sections, (4) Three critical action hyperlinks for promo redemption, subscription transfer, and cancellation. The test uses file references for both setup (login) and navigation (dashboard_overview.yaml), showing a highly modular test design. The verification is split into three stages (TC_plan_details_1, TC_plan_details_2, TC_plan_details_3) with a scroll action between stages 2 and 3, indicating the card content is substantial enough to require scrolling. The test covers two test case IDs (C31165183, C31266443) suggesting it validates both the layout structure and specific content requirements. The validation prompts handle optional content gracefully (remaining free months), making the test robust across different user subscription states.

file_analyses.file_purpose: YAML test case definition for validating the Overview page header content and layout including the printer selector dropdown and the 'Enroll or replace a printer' functionality. This test case (C31165188) validates the header structure with Overview title, instant ink logo, printer selector dropdown with icon and model, and the enroll/replace printer hyperlink. It also validates the modal dialog that appears when clicking the enroll/replace link by comparing against a reference image.

verification_criteria: Modal title matches reference image
All text contents match reference image

Buttons match reference image

Focuses on structural layout and relative positioning rather than pixel-perfect matching or color/font variations. All structural elements must match for validation to pass.

reusable_test_patterns.description: Validates page header in two separate verification steps: first validates title and logo, then validates interactive elements (dropdown and links). Allows for more granular failure detection.

Uses a stored reference image (enroll_or_replace_printer.png) to validate modal content by comparing structural layout, text content, icons, and buttons. Focuses on structural similarity rather than pixel-perfect matching.
Opens modal by clicking link, validates modal content against reference image, then closes modal with Escape key.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test case focuses on the Overview page header structure and the printer enrollment/replacement functionality. It demonstrates: (1) Two-stage header validation splitting title/logo verification from interactive element verification for better failure isolation, (2) Use of reference image comparison for modal validation (enroll_or_replace_printer.png), which is more sophisticated than text-based validation, (3) Validation of dropdown content including both icon and model name, ensuring the dropdown is not just present but properly populated, (4) The reference image comparison explicitly focuses on 'structural layout and relative positioning' rather than 'pixel-level similarity, color shade variations, or font rendering differences', making the test more robust to minor UI changes. The test uses modular file references for login and navigation to the overview page. Note that the modal close step references 'instantink/Signin' page which may be a typo or indicate the modal changes the page context. The test validates a critical user flow for enrolling or replacing printers, which is a key action in the HP Instant Ink service.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This test case validates the Print and Payment History page layout and content structure. It demonstrates: (1) Two-stage validation approach separating header/title validation from interactive elements and bottom sections, (2) Validation of billing cycle information text which is specific to this page, (3) Validation of the Billing Cycle Period section at the bottom of the page, (4) FAQ section interaction and validation to ensure help content is accessible, (5) Consistent header pattern validation matching other HP Instant Ink pages (title, logo, dropdown, enroll link). The test covers two test case IDs (C31266478, C31409335) suggesting it validates both layout structure and specific content requirements. The 30-second wait time after clicking Print and Payment History suggests this page loads substantial billing and print history data. The FAQ validation checks for both 'Frequently Asked Questions' block and 'Print and Payment History' block, suggesting the FAQ may be contextual to the current page. The test uses modular design with file reference for login and logout. This test is important for validating that users can access their billing and print history information and find help through the FAQ section.

key_reusable_test_patterns: AI Visual Verification with Strict Validation — Uses AI-powered screenshot analysis with strict binary validation prompts for complex UI layout validation
Modal Interaction Flow — Opens modal, validates content sections, interacts with elements, closes with Escape
Navigation Menu Expansion Verification — Expands navigation menu and validates all submenu items are present
Systematic Page Navigation and Layout Validation — Navigates through each page systematically validating consistent header patterns
Multi-Stage Visual Verification with Scrolling — Validates content in stages by taking screenshots at different scroll positions
Reference Image Comparison Verification — Uses stored reference images to validate modal content by comparing structural layout
Two-Stage Header Validation — Validates page header in separate steps for title/logo and interactive elements
FAQ Section Interaction and Validation — Clicks FAQ link and validates FAQ blocks are visible
key_ui_elements_validated: Printer Details modal with left and right sections
Status pane with printer model and readiness
HP Instant Ink navigation submenu (Overview, Update Plan, Print and Payment History, Shipment Tracking)
Select printer dropdown with icon and model
Enroll or replace a printer hyperlink
Plan Details card with subscription ID, price, page count, billing and shipping
Action hyperlinks: Redeem Promo, Transfer Subscription, Cancel Instant Ink
pages_covered: instantink/Dashboard — Print and Payment History page
batch_summary.common_verification_approach: All test cases use AI-powered visual verification with strict validation mode. Verification prompts are structured to check for presence and positioning of UI elements, returning {status: false} immediately if any condition fails. Focus is on structural layout and relative positioning rather than pixel-perfect matching.
batch_summary.common_wait_strategy: Sleep-based explicit waits ranging from 1 to 30 seconds depending on expected page load time. Longer waits (20-30 seconds) used after navigation suggesting data-heavy pages or performance considerations.
batch_summary.modular_design_pattern: All test cases use file references for setup (instantservices_login.yaml), navigation (instantservices_dashboard_overview.yaml), and cleanup (instantservices_logout.yaml), demonstrating highly modular test design for reusability.

analysis_notes: Batch 4 contains 5 YAML test case definition files for the HP Instant Ink service within the Instant Services application. All files are test case specifications rather than executable code, defining test flows using a YAML-based test automation framework that supports AI-powered visual verification. Key observations: (1) All test cases follow YAML 1.2 specification version 1.1 format with consistent structure (spec_version, test_case, test_case_id, version, description, flow with phases), (2) Heavy reliance on AI-powered visual verification using screenshot analysis with detailed validation prompts embedded in the YAML, (3) Strict validation mode is used throughout where any missing element causes immediate test failure, (4) Test cases cover comprehensive UI validation across multiple HP Instant Ink pages (Overview, Update Plan, Print and Payment History, Shipment Tracking), (5) Modular design with file references for common operations (login, logout, navigation), (6) Long wait times (20-30 seconds) after navigation suggesting performance considerations or data-heavy pages, (7) Multiple test case IDs often covered in a single YAML file indicating consolidated test scenarios, (8) Validation prompts use f-string formatting suggesting they may be templated with dynamic values at runtime, (9) Reference image comparison used for modal validation (enroll_or_replace_printer.png), (10) Consistent header layout pattern validated across all pages (title, logo, dropdown, links). These YAML files define the test specifications that would be executed by a test automation framework capable of AI-powered visual verification, likely using computer vision and LLM-based validation. The framework appears to support actions like click, verify, escape, vscroll, and can interact with elements by text, label, or visual characteristics.
